purpose: Apply the full ruleset to golf, showcasing the answer-set toolkit. Golf offers an unusually clean bounded set (the four majors) plus countries, scoring terms, the small career-Grand-Slam club, continents, and counts. Deliberately ROTATE answer sets rather than lock on one; lean on reflex-defeaters; avoid the Nicklaus-18 / Tiger-most warhorses. Candidate batch — not yet filed.

answer_set_audit.verdict: 9 questions across 6 enumerable answer sets; largest set = the four majors at 3/9 (33%). No 'which golfer' unbounded space.

candidates.q: Which of golf's four majors is the oldest, first played back in the 1800s?
candidates.note: 1860 — the oldest of the four (and the oldest tournament in golf). Reflex-defeater vs the US Open / Masters. Finalists: the four majors.

candidates.q: Which of the four majors is actually the youngest — the last one created?
candidates.note: 1934. The big surprise: the Masters feels the most storied, but it's the newest major (Open 1860, US Open 1895, PGA 1916, Masters 1934). Strong reflex-defeater.

candidates.q: Three of the majors invite top amateurs to play. Which one is for professionals only?
candidates.note: The only all-professional major; winner takes the Wanamaker Trophy. Finalists: the four majors.

candidates.q: Six men have won all four majors over a career. Which did it at the youngest age?
candidates.note: Completed it at 24 (2000-01). Reflex-defeater vs Nicklaus. Bounded set: Sarazen, Hogan, Player, Nicklaus, Woods, McIlroy (2025).

candidates.q: Who was the very first golfer to win all four majors over his career?
candidates.note: Sealed it at the 1935 Masters (age 33), the same week as his famous albatross at the 15th. Bounded set of six.

candidates.q: How many clubs is a player allowed to carry in the bag during a round?
candidates.note: The Rules of Golf cap it at 14. Answer set: a small number.

candidates.q: A birdie is one under par on a hole and an eagle is two under. What do you call three under on a single hole?
candidates.note: Three under; rarer still is the condor (four under). Bird-themed scoring ladder. The stem gives the pattern, not the answer.

candidates.q: In the Ryder Cup, a team from the United States faces a team representing which continent?
candidates.note: Since 1979 (before that it was Great Britain & Ireland). Answer set: continents.

candidates.q: In which country was the modern game of golf invented, centuries ago?
candidates.note: Home of St Andrews; the game took shape there by the 1400s-1500s. Answer set: countries.
